model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: UConn enters as a 20.5-point favorite and one of the nation's elite programs, posting a 29-5 record as the 2nd-place Big East finisher with an SRS of 22.92 (12th nationally) and a defense ranked 24th in adjusted efficiency (DRtg 97.9) — a level of two-way excellence that Furman's Southern Conference schedule never approached. Furman qualified as the #6 SoCon seed and won the conference tournament as a Cinderella, but their KenPom #191 ranking and negative AdjEM of -1.99 reflect a roster that ranks 200th offensively and 177th defensively, creating a roughly 24-point efficiency gap that is historically insurmountable in a single-elimination game. Dan Hurley's Huskies, two-time defending national champions, carry Big East battle-testing and a near-full-strength roster with Silas Demary Jr. expected to return, and no credible upset path exists for a mid-major automatic qualifier against this caliber of opponent.

reasoningStep.title: UConn vs. Furman — Round of 64 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: UConn is a massive 20.5-point favorite over Furman, backed by elite efficiency metrics (SRS 12th nationally, DRtg 24th) and a 29-5 Big East record. Furman is a Cinderella SoCon champion entering as the #6 conference seed with a KenPom rank of 191 and a negative AdjEM, creating an enormous quality gap. Injury news on Silas Demary Jr. was cautiously optimistic. No credible upset path exists for Furman.
evidence: UConn: 29-5 record, 17-3 Big East, SRS 22.92 (12th nationally), ORtg 116.5 (54th), DRtg 97.9 (24th), SOS 41st nationally, AP Poll #6 as of March 9
Furman: KenPom #191, AdjEM -1.99, AdjO 107.5 (200th), AdjD 109.5; Torvik #195, AdjOE 106.8 (206th), AdjDE 109.0 (177th) — below average on both ends
UConn opened as a 20.5-point favorite with a total of 137.5, one of the largest spreads in the 2026 tournament field per Action Network
Furman entered SoCon Tournament as #6 seed with a 22-12 record and won the championship over ETSU 76-61 — impressive run but against weak competition
Dan Hurley expressed optimism that Silas Demary Jr. would be available for first-round play, keeping UConn near full strength per CT Insider

result.text: From No. 6 Seed to SoCon Champions: Furman’s Incredible Tournament Run » Greenville Sports Media Skip to content March 17, 2026 About Us: GSM Partnered Podcasts Partnered Sites StraightupSports ESPN Radio TikTok Links Greenville Sports Media Covering Sports Everywhere dorseyauto.com Primary Menu About Us: GSM Partnered Podcasts Partnered Sites StraightupSports ESPN Radio TikTok Links Search for: Live Home 2026 March 10 From No. 6 Seed to SoCon Champions: Furman’s Incredible Tournament Run Alex Wilkins Furman Basketball Greenville Sports GSM Southern Conference From No. 6 Seed to SoCon Champions: Furman’s Incredible Tournament Run --> Mark Livingston --> Published: March 10, 2026 | Updated: March 10, 2026 6 minutes read 239 views 0 0 Read Time: 5 Minute, 41 Second From Sixth Seed to SoCon Champions: Furman’s Unforgettable March in Asheville ASHEVILLE, N.C. — March has a way of producing stories that feel almost impossible while they are unfolding. Over the weekend inside Harrah’s Cherokee Center , the Furman Paladins men’s basketball authored one of those stories. Entering the Southern Conference Tournament as the No. 6 seed, Furman faced a daunting road. The bracket required the Paladins to win three games in three days, a challenge that demands depth, composure, and a little bit of March magic. Nevertheless, as the weekend progressed, Furman steadily transformed from a dangerous underdog into the team nobody could stop. Momentum built with each passing game. Confidence grew with every clutch basket and defensive stop. By the time the final horn sounded on championship night, the Paladins had completed a remarkable climb from sixth seed to conference champions — a run defined by resilience, timely performances, and a team that refused to believe its season was finished. A Team That Found Its Rhythm Tournament basketball often reveals a team’s true character. Early in the weekend, Furman showed that it had both the patience and the poise to navigate the chaos that inevitably comes with postseason play. Rather than relying on one player to carry the offense, the Paladins leaned on ball movement and spacing to create opportunities. Possessions rarely felt rushed. Instead, the offense flowed through multiple passes until a good shot became a great one. Meanwhile, defensively, Furman stayed disciplined, forcing opponents into difficult looks and battling for rebounds that often ignited transition opportunities. As a result, the Paladins gradually se

result.title: What UConn's Dan Hurley said about Silas Demary Jr.'s injury status
result.snippet: The Huskies have now fallen short by one game in their hunt for both a Big East regular-season and conference tournament title, now awaiting their fate in the NCAA Tournament on Selection Sunday. Hurley expressed optimism that Demary will be available as first-round play begins next week.

result.text: From No. 6 Seed to SoCon Champions: Furman’s Incredible Tournament Run » Greenville Sports Media Skip to content March 17, 2026 About Us: GSM Partnered Podcasts Partnered Sites StraightupSports ESPN Radio TikTok Links Greenville Sports Media Covering Sports Everywhere dorseyauto.com Primary Menu About Us: GSM Partnered Podcasts Partnered Sites StraightupSports ESPN Radio TikTok Links Search for: Live Home 2026 March 10 From No. 6 Seed to SoCon Champions: Furman’s Incredible Tournament Run Alex Wilkins Furman Basketball Greenville Sports GSM Southern Conference From No. 6 Seed to SoCon Champions: Furman’s Incredible Tournament Run --> Mark Livingston --> Published: March 10, 2026 | Updated: March 10, 2026 6 minutes read 239 views 0 0 Read Time: 5 Minute, 41 Second From Sixth Seed to SoCon Champions: Furman’s Unforgettable March in Asheville ASHEVILLE, N.C. — March has a way of producing stories that feel almost impossible while they are unfolding. Over the weekend inside Harrah’s Cherokee Center , the Furman Paladins men’s basketball authored one of those stories. Entering the Southern Conference Tournament as the No. 6 seed, Furman faced a daunting road. The bracket required the Paladins to win three games in three days, a challenge that demands depth, composure, and a little bit of March magic. Nevertheless, as the weekend progressed, Furman steadily transformed from a dangerous underdog into the team nobody could stop. Momentum built with each passing game. Confidence grew with every clutch basket and defensive stop. By the time the final horn sounded on championship night, the Paladins had completed a remarkable climb from sixth seed to conference champions — a run defined by resilience, timely performances, and a team that refused to believe its season was finished. A Team That Found Its Rhythm Tournament basketball often reveals a team’s true character. Early in the weekend, Furman showed that it had both the patience and the poise to navigate the chaos that inevitably comes with postseason play. Rather than relying on one player to carry the offense, the Paladins leaned on ball movement and spacing to create opportunities. Possessions rarely felt rushed. Instead, the offense flowed through multiple passes until a good shot became a great one. Meanwhile, defensively, Furman stayed disciplined, forcing opponents into difficult looks and battling for rebounds that often ignited transition opportunities.
